channel.title: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value
channel.description: i have a component that allows a user to select an option from a dropdown menu. The user's selected is being set to localStorage, but i am wanting that localStorage value to be accessible to all other components in the project. I am thinking the way to make it globally accessible is to set a state.theme value equal to the value of the localStorage value. do you think this is an appropriate way of accomplishing the goal, and if so, how would i set a state.theme value to the localStorage value?

item.title: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.source: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value

item.title: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
I’m closing this thread because this is a duplicated topic with

Adding localStorage data to a state.theme value 🤗 Dev Talk & Questions
Hi @meaghan1, You could set an action to modify that value in the state while writing that value in localStorage. https://docs.frontity.org/learning-frontity/actions You also have to take into account that every time a page is reload the values stored in memory will be erased. So, you can use some of the “Actions triggered by Frontity” (afterCSR → afterClientSideRendering) to recover this data eery time a page is loaded https://docs.frontity.org/learning-frontity/actions#actions-triggered-by…

Answer to the questions provided there

Read full topic
item.source: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value

item.title: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value
item.dc:creator: furrysmile2
item.description:
state.theme.myValue = localStorage.getItem(‘myValue’), in afterCSR.
Also useEffect to update, if needed.

Read full topic
item.source: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value

item.title: Adding localStorage data to state.theme value
item.dc:creator: meaghan1
item.description:
i have a component that allows a user to select an option from a dropdown menu. The user’s selected is being set to localStorage, but i am wanting that localStorage value to be accessible to all other components in the project. I am thinking the way to make it globally accessible is to set a state.theme value equal to the value of the localStorage value. do you think this is an appropriate way of accomplishing the goal, and if so, how would i set a state.theme value to the localStorage value?
